title: llm_providers
description: Tier 1 SOT — API credentials per key. FROZEN from system mutation (only admin/Bos can add/delete records). Each (provider, account_email) tuple is a separate record; multiple records per provider is allowed by design (parallel keys for capacity). NOT a duplicate-detection target. Schema strictly enforces required fields and enum membership (verified 2026-06-23 against live data; 16-field actual usage).

account_email.description: Account identifier — email, 'owner', or 'email/key_type' for openrouter

api_key.description: Actual API key (masked in some contexts). IMMUTABLE — sot_api_key_middleware rejects $set.api_key writes.

is_active.description: Runtime authority — admin-controlled. True = eligible for SOT T1/T2/T3 routing dispatch. False = excluded from routing even if key_status=VALID. is_active and key_status are independent axes: a key can be VALID but inactive (replacement pending), or UNVERIFIED but active=placed-in-rotation.

key_status.description: 1-shot probe result (Bos 2026-06-23): one HTTP probe + parseable body ⇒ VALID; non-2xx ⇒ status-specific error; transport failure ⇒ TIMEOUT/UNREACHABLE/DNS_FAIL. Not polled continuously — single probe is sufficient.

key_purpose.description:
Role of this key. SINGLE-PURPOSE providers use one key for everything. MULTI-PURPOSE providers (notably openrouter) have separate keys for different surfaces:
- 'inference' — call/invoke models
- 'provisioning' — listing/management/admin
- 'primary' / 'secondary' / 'experimental' — paralel keys for capacity
Misusing the wrong key on the wrong surface yields auth failure or wrong data.

free_bypass.description: T1 provider-level FREE override: True ⇒ every model under this provider is forced is_free=true (e.g. minimax is upstream-paid but Bos set free_bypass=true). False ⇒ per-model billing policy. The single provider-level free control (supersedes T2 force_free).

restored_at.description: When this record was last restored via sot_reverse_restore / historical audit. Stored as either ISO-8601 string OR BSON datetime (Mongo ships datetimes as native types, so validators see datetime objects).

verified_at.description: Last key probe timestamp (set by ilma_validate_keys / similar probes). May be BSON datetimeobject in DB.

last_valid_evidence.description: Human-readable trace of the last probe (URL, HTTP code, evidence type, timestamp)

note.description: Free-text note (audit remarks, restoration context, deprecation notice)

_schema_evolution.v1_2026_06_19: Initial 9-field schema: provider, account_email, api_key, key_status, key_purpose, account_status, added, added_by, free_bypass.
_schema_evolution.v2_2026_06_23: Schema widened to 17 fields after audit revealed drift (REC #4). Added: is_active (mandatory for T1 gate), restored_at/by (audit provenance), verified_at/by + last_valid_evidence + validation_method (REC #1 probe-result fields), note (free-text). Enum key_status gained 'UNREACHABLE' (separate from TIMEOUT). Compatibility preserved: previously-valid docs remain valid; only new optional fields added.
_schema_evolution.no_removal: No fields removed — only added. Backwards compatible.

_migration_note_2026_06_19: Pre-v1 schema had 23+ fields (auth_format, base_url, free_tier, endpoints, payload_format). Those are now consolidated in providers collection (Tier 2) and should no longer be stored here.
_migration_note_2026_06_23: REC #4 — schema extended (NOT narrowed) to match actual data. Existing docs remain valid. New validator passes 0/23 invalid (validated via validators/validate_llm_providers.py --mongo).
